$comment: audit-allowlist.json — npm audit CVE/GHSA allowlist for kithkit.
Format: each entry in 'allowlist' MUST have:
id      — the advisory identifier, e.g. 'GHSA-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx' or 'CVE-2024-12345'
expires — ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which the entry is considered stale and audit-check fails
reason  — human-readable justification for why this advisory is accepted as unreachable
The audit-check.mjs wrapper reads this file and suppresses any npm audit advisory whose id
matches a non-expired entry. Any advisory NOT listed here causes a non-zero exit.
An expired entry that is still relied upon also causes a non-zero exit.
IMPORTANT: This file is human-curated. Entries must be reviewed periodically.
Set expires to the earliest date you can commit to re-evaluating the risk.
Run the check: npm run audit:check
Populate with: npm audit --json | jq '.vulnerabilities' (identify GHSA IDs to evaluate)

allowlist.reason: Transitive uuid advisory (buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided) pulled via botframework-connector/msal. Unreachable in kithkit: no direct uuid dependency, no uuid imports in daemon/src, and UUIDs are generated exclusively via node:crypto randomUUID (41 call sites) - the vulnerable uuid v3/v5/v6 buf code path is never executed. Accepted as unreachable until the upstream SDK bumps its uuid transitive. Re-evaluate by 2026-12-12.

Transitive esbuild advisory (missing binary integrity verification in the Deno module, enabling RCE via a malicious NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY at install time). esbuild is build/dev-only, pulled transitively via kithkit-a2a-client -> tsx -> esbuild@0.28.0; NOT a direct or runtime daemon dependency (absent from package.json deps, not imported in daemon/src). The vector requires installing from an attacker-controlled npm registry; kithkit installs are controlled (trusted registry, no NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY override in CI/deploy), so it is unreachable. Accepted as unreachable until the upstream tsx transitive bumps esbuild. Re-evaluate by 2026-12-15.

Transitive esbuild advisory (arbitrary file read when running esbuild's development server on Windows). esbuild is build/dev-only, pulled transitively via kithkit-a2a-client -> tsx -> esbuild@0.28.0; NOT a direct or runtime daemon dependency. kithkit never runs esbuild's dev server, and the fleet is macOS (the advisory is Windows-specific) - doubly unreachable. Accepted as unreachable until the upstream tsx transitive bumps esbuild. Re-evaluate by 2026-12-15.
